note: Approved templates only (90 of 181). Field/slot schema parsed from the annotated SVG data-attributes; the SVG files are the render source of truth. Fit metadata re-baked 2026-07-14 (verify/bake-fit.js, row-true): data-w/minfont/maxlines + capacity-true maxLens.

templates.useWhen: Clean dark styling and every visible element (eyebrow, headline, subhead, body card, 4 chips) maps to a field, but the layout renders subhead and body-1 as near-identical adjacent paragraphs and leaves the bottom ~25% plus the card interior empty, making it feel awkward and redundant next to stronger simple-text siblings.
Clean, on-brand numbered-point slide SemiAnalysis would use constantly in list carousels; ship it after exposing the "01" numeral as an editable number field.

Clean on-brand hook cover whose three visible texts (eyebrow, headline, SWIPE label) all map to fields and the empty lower third is the module drop slot, though as-rendered that unfilled band leaves the composition bottom-heavy with dead space.

On-brand terminal stat-readout card with full field coverage (4 metric rows + eyebrow/headline/caption all editable, terminal covered by module slot); only flaw is the ~26% dead band between headline and terminal, which keeps it at 4/5 but it belongs in the regular-use stat-card set.

templates.fixNote: flag traced to a render artifact: the reviewer look pane used the legacy overlay renderer; the baked template is clean (evidence _qa/shots/look/94.png)
templates.useWhen: ClusterMAX-style graded scorecard that SA would use constantly and parses fully (all 13 fields map, module slot covers the card area); only fixes needed are closing the ~18% dead gap under the headline and centering grade letters in their chips.

Clean on-brand quote card (amber quote ornament, eyebrow, headline-quote, avatar+attribution all map to fields with an image slot) — a staple format worth publishing, docked one point for the empty bottom third when the quote is short.

Clean on-brand TAM/SAM funnel whose 11 fields exactly cover all visible text (4 tier label/value pairs + eyebrow/headline/caption); keep for market-sizing cards, though the bottom "Won" tier's muddy brown lowers label contrast slightly.
On-brand ranked-bar leaderboard (a regular SA comparison device) with fully mapped fields; only fix is closing the ~22% empty band between headline and first row.

On-brand stat+chart hero with full field coverage (stat, delta chip, subhead, 3 footer stats, chart slot); only flaw is the ~16% dead band between chart and footer row that leaves the bottom stats floating — ship it, optionally tighten that gap.

Clean on-brand supplier-share bar comparison (a staple SA device) with all 11 visible text regions mapping to fields and the bar area slot-covered; only nit is slightly loose vertical spacing between bar sections.

Clean, on-brand end/CTA card SA would use in nearly every carousel — keep in core but wire the Subscribe CTA text to an editable field and tighten the empty bottom quarter where the frame stops well short of the canvas.

Clean on-brand end/follow card where every visible element (eyebrow, headline, subhead, three label/handle rows, logo) maps to a field — a staple closer for every carousel, docked from 5 only for the empty bottom band and the dashed frame line clipping through the social rows.

Clean, on-brand end/CTA signoff (kicker + wordmark + takeaway + URL) with every visible text region editable; generous top/bottom whitespace keeps it at 4 rather than 5, but SA would use this on nearly every carousel.

Clean, on-brand next-part teaser card that SA's multi-part series need regularly, with all visible text (eyebrow/headline/subhead/part-pill) mapped to fields; only nit is the floating PART pill and generous empty top/bottom bands, which read as intentional end-card breathing room.

Clean, fully-editable end/CTA card (eyebrow/headline/pill all mapped, dashed module slot present) with intentional-feeling whitespace; only nit is the quote-mark ornament reading slightly quote-card rather than question-prompt.

Polished cobalt-amber race-bar chart card where bar widths track the stat values and every visible text (eyebrow, headline, 5 label/stat pairs, footer) maps to a listed field — exactly the kind of comparison stat card SemiAnalysis would reach for regularly.

Clean on-brand quad-stat card with all 10 visible texts mapped to fields (stat cards are a core analyst need); only softness is the ~20% dead band when the module slot is unfilled, and one stat value awkwardly rides a label-* role.

Clean, on-brand myth-vs-reality comparison card with every visible text mapped to a field and the empty bottom covered by the module slot; only flaw is loose vertical rhythm (dead band between subhead and cards), a natural fit for SA's debunk-style analysis carousels.

Clean on-brand sign-off/end card with every visible text mapped to a field and the seal covered by the module slot; only knock is the slightly hollow oversized seal panel (17.6% dead band), so ship it as the standard closer.

Renders as an unfinished skeleton — four empty ghost panels, 33% dead band, and body text clipped at the bottom-right edge — with 3 of 4 panel regions lacking drop slots, so it fails both looks and articulation.
Clean on-brand spec-sheet "save this" stat card whose visible text maps 1:1 to its 11 fields; only flaw is sparse vertical coverage (~35% of rows), which the centered card composition carries.

Strong on-brand type-slab cover with every visible element mapped to a field and the dead band above the headline covered by the module drop slot, though it reads slightly hollow when that slot is empty.
Distinctive on-brand dossier cover with all visible text mapped to fields; ship it, but nudge the CONFIDENTIAL stamp so it stops fully obscuring the CLEARANCE label.
Clean, on-brand giant-quote card with every visible element editable — core quote format; only nits are a sparse lower half and a stray collapsed stat/divider tick at mid-left when stats are empty.

Clean on-brand slopegraph for rank/share-shift stories (a recurring SA motif) with all 30 visible text pieces (2 column labels, 5x names/ranks/percentages per side, footer) mapping to the field roles and the chart area slot-covered; only nit is the airy dead band between headline and chart panel, and note the line geometry is baked so re-ranked data needs the module swapped, not just text edits.

Clean, on-brand dumbbell comparison (exactly the stat/chart card SA reaches for) with every visible text mapping 1:1 to the 20 fields, but tighten bottom padding — the fifth row crowds the canvas edge and the SEMIANALYSIS.COM footer overlaps its track.

Clean on-brand bullet-vs-target stat card (a device SA would use regularly for yield/utilization-vs-plan) with every visible text mapped to a field, but ship after fixing the bottom edge: the fourth card and TARGET/YIELD footer legend are clipped at the canvas boundary.

Clean on-brand supply/demand tornado chart card with every label, category, and value mapped to editable fields — a regular-use SA stat/comparison layout; only caveat is bar widths aren't data-bound to the stat fields.
Polished, well-balanced pros/cons comparison card where all 14 visible text regions map cleanly to field roles — a pattern SemiAnalysis would use constantly for trade-off analysis slides.

On-brand head-to-head comparison (cobalt vs amber spec cards with winner badge) that SemiAnalysis would use constantly for GPU/ASIC-style matchups and parses cleanly into its 19 fields; only nit is the roomy empty card interiors when the module slot is unfilled.

Clean on-brand HBM spec-comparison card whose 18 fields map 1:1 to every visible text region (headers, 12 table cells, pill, footer) — a format SemiAnalysis uses constantly; only nit is airy dead space between headline and table.

Polished issue-colophon cover/end card whose every visible text maps to a field and whose empty lower band is the module drop slot — ships as a regular-use cover once that slot is filled, docking one point for the airy bottom third when left empty.

Clean on-brand 3x2 stat wall whose 15 visible texts map one-to-one to the 15 fields with a module slot covering the empty band — a regular-use stat card, only slightly hurt by the unpopulated slot's dead zone under the grid.

Distinctive gradient record-banner stat card that maps every visible text region to its 14 fields and fits SA's regular stat-card use; slight vertical airiness (12% dead band) keeps it at 4 rather than 5.

Clean, on-brand Gantt/swimlane timeline (amber-cobalt bars, NOW marker) that SA would reach for regularly on ramp/roadmap cards; all 9 visible text regions plus eyebrow/headline/footer map to the 16 fields and the chart area is the module slot — only caveat is bar spans are fixed geometry, not data-editable.

Clean gate-chain status card (A-D stages with PASS/BLOCKED/PENDING pills) is a natural SA supply-chain bottleneck device; all 12 row texts plus eyebrow/headline/footer map to the 15 fields and the badge maps to the module slot, with only a slightly large bottom dead band keeping it from a 5.

Clean stepped build-stack timeline with status chips that SA would reuse for buildout/roadmap analyses — every visible text (eyebrow, headline, 5x stat/body/label, footer) maps to a field — though the ~17% dead band between headline and the stack keeps it from a 5.

On-brand die-floorplan breakdown card whose 22 fields exactly cover all visible text (8 label/value blocks, legend as stats, eyebrow/headline/footer); ship it, noting block areas are fixed so edited percentages won't re-proportion the treemap.

Polished on-brand wafer/stat-card layout (module drop slot + three amber stat tiles) that SA would reuse for yield/chip economics posts; ship it after adding one more field so all nine stat-card texts are editable.

On-brand log-scale bandwidth-ladder chart card with clean field mapping (all rung labels/stats/units editable) — a regular-use SA chart module; just fix the clipped dark "GB/s" unit overflowing the shortest (NVMe) bar and tighten the headline-to-chart gap.

Render looks broken — the promised 'one sentence' payoff never appears and the lower two-thirds is filled with two empty gradient slabs (only one backed by a slot) plus a highlight chip that awkwardly clips the headline's first word, so cut despite the clean eyebrow/headline/footer mapping.

Both panels render mostly empty (17.8% content coverage, ~24% dead band) — headline floats in a half-empty card and the stat card has a huge void below "Power per rack / 5GW", so despite clean type and full field mapping it reads as broken/unfinished next to stronger stat-card siblings.

Polished pushback-vs-our-answer rebuttal card with all seven visible text regions cleanly mapped to fields — a format SA analysis carousels use constantly; only nits are the 14% headline-to-panel gap and slightly offset dashed frames near the footer.

Clean on-brand 3-point takeaways list with strong numeral hierarchy and every visible text region mapped to a field (12 fields cover eyebrow/headline/numbers/headings/bodies/footer, badge covered by module slot); only nit is slightly airy space under item 3.
Roughly a third of the canvas is an empty void inside the margin-note panel with a stray orphaned gradient swatch, so despite clean field mapping and a strong header the card renders as unfinished.

Polished on-brand thesis/standfirst opener (drop-cap, byline, min-read stat) where every visible text maps to a field role — a staple SA carousel slide, only docked for the slightly awkward two-line headline wrap and orphaned last body line.

Distinctive on-brand blueprint cover — all 10 visible text regions (eyebrow, headline, 6 diagram callouts, spec line, footer) map to fields and the chip cross-section sits in the module slot; lower half is slightly airy (12% dead band) but it ships as a regular-use cover.

Striking on-brand die-shot cover in the cobalt-amber palette with all five visible text regions (eyebrow, headline, three legend labels) mapped to fields and the die graphic covered by the module slot — a natural repeat-use hero for SA's chip coverage, docked from 5 only for the slightly busy grid and small legend swatches.

Strong on-brand cover card — the contour-star hero plus eyebrow/headline/subhead/footer all map to listed fields with real content sitting naturally; only nit is the headline slightly overlapping contour lines, so a touch of scrim behind the title would make it a 5.

Clean on-brand cover — kicker/title/byline all map to the 3 body fields over sanctioned aura-glow gradient; generous but intentional whitespace, only nit is the faint dashed frame outline and a title that didn't match the injected article's headline.

Polished on-brand circuit-hero cover with all visible text mapped to editable fields; only flaw is the blue trace node clipping the eyebrow text near "GRAB" — nudge trace endpoints clear of the card and it ships.

On-brand chip-comparison bubble-chart card whose eyebrow, headline, 6 bubble labels, axes, legend, and body all map to fields with a module slot over the chart; only the dead band between headline and chart panel keeps it from a 5.

Clean on-brand waffle stat card with coherent 78%/78-cell data story and every visible text region mapped to a field plus a module slot for the grid; only nits are the header-to-waffle dead band and legend swatches floating above their labels.

On-brand marimekko market-share card with every label/stat/legend/footer mapped to the 26 fields and a module slot on the chart; only nits are the headline-to-chart dead band and the Enterprise header hugging the right edge — a chart type SemiAnalysis would use constantly.

Strong on-brand small-multiples stat dashboard SA would use regularly — ship it, but add per-card sparkline slots (or neutral placeholder lines) since 5 of 6 charts are baked-in, and clean the dashed/clipped outline at the right and bottom edges.

On-brand treemap stat card whose 14 tile labels/values map exactly to stat-1..13 + body-1 — ideal for SA cost/BOM breakdowns; just fix the stray dashed guide line crossing the top tiles and the near-clipped "Storage" label, and note tile sizes won't reflow when percentages are edited.

On-brand dark streamgraph chart card that SA would use constantly — every visible text maps (eyebrow, headline, 5 axis labels, 8 legend name/percent bodies, footer) and the chart region is the module slot; docked at 4 because the SEMIANALYSIS.COM footer crowds the lower-left legend row and headline-to-chart whitespace is a touch loose.
